name: Super Bowl
description: The NFL's championship game — history, MVPs, halftime shows

questions.q: Which team won the very first Super Bowl in January 1967?

source.notes: Defeated Kansas City Chiefs 35-10. Coached by Vince Lombardi.

questions.q: Who won Super Bowl III in 1969, causing one of the biggest upsets in sports history?

source.notes: Joe Namath famously 'guaranteed' the Jets' win, defeating the heavily favored Colts 16-7.

questions.q: Which quarterback threw 5 touchdown passes in Super Bowl XXIV (1990), a record at the time?

source.notes: 49ers defeated Broncos 55-10. Montana also won the game's MVP.

questions.q: In Super Bowl XXXVIII (2004), what notorious incident happened during the halftime show?

source.notes: Justin Timberlake exposed Janet Jackson during 'Rock Your Body.' Led to FCC fines and major changes to live broadcasts.

questions.q: The winner of the Super Bowl receives which trophy?

source.notes: Named after Vince Lombardi in 1971, after his death. Made by Tiffany & Co.

questions.q: Which famous commercial director helmed Apple's iconic '1984' ad that aired during Super Bowl XVIII?

source.notes: Launched the Macintosh. The ad only aired once nationally. Regularly called the greatest commercial ever.

questions.q: Which team has lost the most Super Bowls without a win?

source.notes: Vikings: 0-4. Bills: 0-4 (four consecutive, 1991-1994). Both have the same record.

questions.q: Who was the first quarterback to win Super Bowls with three different teams?

source.notes: Peyton Manning won with Colts and Broncos (2 teams). No QB has won with 3 different teams.

questions.q: Which quarterback has won the most Super Bowl rings (7)?

source.notes: 6 with Patriots (2001, 2003, 2004, 2014, 2016, 2018) and 1 with Buccaneers (2020).

questions.q: Super Bowl LV (2021) was the first Super Bowl where which notable thing happened?
choices: A team played on its home field

source.notes: Tampa Bay Buccaneers played at Raymond James Stadium, their home field. They won (obviously).

questions.q: Which city hosted the first Super Bowl ever played?

source.notes: Memorial Coliseum in Los Angeles, January 15, 1967.

questions.q: Which NFL team has the most Super Bowl wins (6, tied)?

source.notes: Steelers: SB IX, X, XIII, XIV, XL, XLIII. Patriots match at 6.

questions.q: What year did the Super Bowl move to being played in February instead of January?

source.notes: Super Bowl XXXVI in Feb 2002. Schedule shifted after the post-9/11 season.

questions.q: Who was MVP of Super Bowl XLI (2007), in which Tony Dungy became the first Black head coach to win a Super Bowl?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: rewrote stem so the relative clause clearly attaches to Dungy (the coach) rather than implying Manning was the African-American coach. Peyton Manning won MVP; Tony Dungy coached Indianapolis Colts to the win.

questions.q: Which NFL MVP is known for the phrase 'Omaha!' called at the line of scrimmage?

source.notes: Used as an audible indicator at the line. Became a cultural catchphrase.

questions.q: Which team won Super Bowl XLVIII (2014), blowing out the Denver Broncos 43-8?

source.notes: Led by Russell Wilson and the 'Legion of Boom' defense. Safety on first play.

questions.q: In Super Bowl LI (2017), which team came back from a 28-3 deficit to win?

source.notes: Patriots vs Falcons. First Super Bowl to go to overtime. Final score: 34-28.

questions.q: What is the name of the halftime show performer who delivered a career-defining performance at Super Bowl XLIX (2015)?

source.notes: Katy Perry. Also included 'Left Shark' — the backup dancer who went viral.

questions.q: Which team won Super Bowl LVIII (2024), their third championship in 5 years?

source.notes: Defeated 49ers 25-22 in overtime. Patrick Mahomes won his third SB MVP.

questions.q: Approximately how many chicken wings do Americans consume on Super Bowl Sunday, per the National Chicken Council?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced tautological 'name of the tradition' question with the wing-count factoid. Super Bowl Sunday is the second-highest food-consumption day in the US after Thanksgiving (also ~12.5M pizzas, ~150M hot dogs).
